title: Relativistic theory for coupled orbital and spin angular momentum dynamics in magnetic systems
authors: Subhadip Santra, Ritwik Mondal, Marco Berritta, Peter M. Oppeneer
abstract: We develop a complete relativistic theory to describe the dynamics of electronic angular momentum including both spin (S) and orbital (L) contributions in magnetic systems. We start with the relativistic Dirac-Kohn-Sham Hamiltonian under the influence of an electromagnetic field and apply a unitary transformation to formulate the extended Pauli Hamiltonian. Using the transformed semirelativistic Hamiltonian, we derive the angular momentum dynamics for the orbital and spin angular momenta. Thereby, we formulate the coupled dynamics of orbital and spin moments consistent with the relativistic Dirac framework. Considering especially the conservation of the total angular momentum, J = S +L, we show first that J is conserved in the absence of a spin-polarized Kohn-Sham exchange field, but is no longer conserved under the application of an electromagnetic field, e.g., laser pulse, THz field, etc. Second, considering magnetic systems with atomic spin and orbital momenta, we derive the coupled equations of motion of angular momenta dynamics whilst making the atomistic Heisenberg approximation for the exchange interaction. Our results suggest that, under these assumptions, the total angular momentum remains conserved, even with electromagnetic field, but atomic spin and orbital angular momenta individually are not conserved.
summary: This paper provides a relativistic theoretical framework to describe how spin and orbital angular momentum interact and redistribute in magnetic materials. It highlights how the choice of exchange-interaction model (general vs. atomistic) fundamentally changes the predicted conservation laws for total angular momentum during ultrafast processes.
main_problem: The paper seeks to develop a complete relativistic theory for the coupled dynamics of spin and orbital angular momentum in magnetic systems, specifically addressing the conservation or non-conservation of total angular momentum under external electromagnetic fields.
main_result: The authors show that while total angular momentum (J) is conserved in the absence of a spin-polarized exchange field, it is not conserved under external electromagnetic fields (like laser pulses); however, under an atomistic Heisenberg approximation, J remains conserved even with external fields.
method: The study employs a relativistic framework starting from the Dirac-Kohn-Sham Hamiltonian, applying a Foldy-Wouthuysen unitary transformation to derive an extended Pauli Hamiltonian and using Heisenberg equations of motion to derive angular momentum dynamics.
model_or_system: The system consists of electrons in magnetic and nonmagnetic materials (including ferromagnets and alloys) subjected to external electromagnetic fields, modeled using the Dirac-Kohn-Sham and extended Pauli Hamiltonians.
key_observables: Spin angular momentum (S), orbital angular momentum (L), and total angular momentum (J = S + L).
important_parameters: Relativistic corrections (1/c^2 order), electromagnetic field strength (E and B fields), spin-polarized Kohn-Sham exchange field (Bxc), and ultrafast/femtosecond timescales.
main_assumptions: The derivation relies on a semirelativistic 1/c^2 expansion, a weak-field approximation, and compares results between a general Kohn-Sham exchange field and an atomistic Heisenberg approximation.
paper_structure: The paper begins with the derivation of the relativistic Hamiltonian via unitary transformation, proceeds to analyze the dynamics of L and S in nonmagnetic and magnetic systems, investigates conservation laws under external fields, and concludes with a comparison between general exchange and atomistic Heisenberg models.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
